lesson.menu_desc: Adjective order & be like / look like -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 11 -- Describing People -- Adjective order and be like / look like
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carolina Paludetto Rodrigues | Aula 11 -- Describing People

listenings.text: Today I want to introduce a character from a new story. Her name is Clara, and she is fifteen years old. Clara is a tall, cheerful girl with long curly hair and a few freckles on her face. She looks like her grandmother in the old family photos. But what is she really like? Clara is very generous and polite. She always shares her lunch with her friends and helps the new students at school. She is also brave, because she is never afraid to ask questions in class. Some people think she is a little shy at first, but when you know her, she talks and laughs all day. A good character is not only a face. A good character has a personality. So when you describe a person, tell us how they look and what they are like.

Hi, this is Carolina, and I want to tell you about my best friend, Marina. Marina is a short, cheerful girl with straight brown hair and big brown eyes. She looks like her mother, and they have the same lovely smile. But the best part of Marina is her personality. She is very generous and always shares her snacks with me. She is also polite and kind to everyone at school. Marina is a little shy with new people, but she is very brave on the handball court. When I write a story, I sometimes think about Marina, because she is a wonderful person to describe. A good friend is easy to describe, because you know how they look and what they are like.

extra_audio.text: Carolina wants to create a new character for her story. First, she chooses the hair: long and curly. Then she adds the face, with a few freckles. After that, she thinks about the personality, generous and brave. Finally, she writes the name of the character at the top of the page.
